articles.title: ICE Agent Charged in Shooting of a Venezuelan Immigrant in Minnesota
articles.summary: The agent is the second federal officer to face felony charges in Minnesota stemming from Operation Metro Surge, the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown.

articles.title: Federal Prosecutors Move to Drop Bribery Case Against Indian Magnate
articles.summary: Gautam Adani had been accused of lying to American investors about payoffs in India. The end of the case came as President Trump has signaled a transactional approach to justice.

articles.title: Trump Says He Authorized New Strikes on Iran, but Has Decided to Hold Off
articles.summary: President Trump has repeatedly threatened new military action against Iran, only to pull back from resuming major fighting in a deeply unpopular, expensive conflict.

articles.title: Tuesday is a big primary day. Here's what you need to know
articles.summary: Six states are holding primaries May 19 that could help to decide the balance of power in Congress and in key state governments.

articles.title: What is Trump’s $1.776 Billion ‘Anti-Weaponization’ Fund?
articles.summary: The Trump administration is creating a $1.8 billion fund to compensate people it says were wronged by the federal government, a group that could be largely made up of the president’s allies.

articles.title: This Ebola outbreak raises questions about when it all began — and the U.S. response
articles.summary: The sheer number of cases and deaths are a sign that the outbreak might have been smoldering before the virus was identified.

articles.title: What to Know About Elon Musk’s Trial Against OpenAI
articles.summary: A jury in Oakland, Calif., reached a decision after a three-week-long trial seen as pivotal for the future of OpenAI and the artificial intelligence race.

articles.title: Jury dismisses all claims in Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI CEO Sam Altman
articles.summary: Musk had sought to oust Altman from his leadership position over claims that he and others breached their duty to OpenAI's original nonprofit mission and unjustly enriched themselves.

articles.title: NPR trims jobs in newsroom overhaul as it confronts era without public funding
articles.summary: NPR is offering buyouts to journalists as it overhauls its newsroom, with the threat of layoffs to follow. Two recent gifts totaling $113 million are primarily dedicated to NPR's tech infrastructure.

articles.title: Son of radicals, Zayd Ayers Dohrn details a childhood underground and on the run
articles.summary: Dohrn's parents, Bill Ayers and Bernardine Dohrn, helped found the the Weather Underground. "I knew that the FBI was chasing us," he says. His memoir is Dangerous, Dirty, Violent, and Young.

articles.title: Abortion Pill Lawsuit Leaves Trump in a Political Bind Ahead of the Midterms
articles.summary: Louisiana wants the Food and Drug Administration to curtail access to the medication. Doing so could cost Republicans at the polls.

articles.title: The Supreme Court avoids taking up a fight over Voting Rights Act enforcement for now
articles.summary: After recently weakening the Voting Rights Act, the Supreme Court avoided for now taking up a legal question that may severely limit enforcement of the law's remaining protections for minority voters.

articles.title: Trump drops IRS lawsuit, paving the way for a settlement
articles.summary: The president sued the IRS and the Treasury Department in January, demanding $10 billion over the leak of his tax returns years ago.

articles.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy loses primary. And, WHO declares Ebola outbreak a global emergency
articles.summary: Sen. Bill Cassidy, who voted to convict Trump, lost the Republican primary in Louisiana. And, the WHO has declared a global health emergency over a new Ebola outbreak.

articles.title: Pop star Shakira is acquitted in a Spanish tax fraud case
articles.summary: The decision follows years of tax troubles in Spain for the Colombian superstar. Spanish tax authorities did not prove that the singer was a resident of Spain, the court said in its decision.

articles.title: Trump’s Approval Sinks Amid Unpopular War, Darkening G.O.P. Prospects
articles.summary: With the midterms nearing, President Trump’s approval rating has hit a second-term low as voters question his handling of the economy, according to the latest New York Times/Siena poll.

articles.title: Ukraine launches large-scale drone strikes on Russia
articles.summary: As peace talks stall, Ukraine launches one of the biggest drone strikes against Russia since the beginning of Russia's full-scale invasion in an attempt to weaken Russia's war machine.

articles.title: North Korea is relieved its nuclear weapons have prevented a fate like Iran's
articles.summary: The U.S. long pushed for both Iran and North Korea to quit their nuclear plans. After seeing Iran's leader get killed, North Korea's Kim Jong Un believes he made the right choice to stay the course.

articles.title: U.S. and Israel prepare for potential return to full-scale war with Iran
articles.summary: Events over the weekend highlight the risk of renewed war, as the United States and Israel signal they would be ready to fight Iran again.

articles.title: Peru's electoral board confirms June 7 presidential runoff
articles.summary: Authorities confirmed on Sunday the official results of the first round of the presidential elections in early April, with Keiko Fujimori and Roberto Sánchez advancing to the runoff on June 7.

articles.title: Drone strikes UAE nuclear plant highlighting risk of renewed war
articles.summary: A drone strike sparked a fire on the edge of the United Arab Emirates' sole nuclear power plant on Sunday in what authorities called an "unprovoked terrorist attack."

articles.title: Executions nearly doubled in the U.S. last year, and soared abroad
articles.summary: The number of people killed in state-sanctioned executions worldwide rose to a 44-year high in 2025, according to a new report from Amnesty International.

articles.title: Tonys 2026 Predictions: Who Will Win? And Who Should?
articles.summary: Our chief theater critic looks at this year’s nominees and makes some predictions (and recommendations).

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
